title: poeciloneuron indicum
intro: Poeciloneuron indicum, also known as the Indian tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae, native to the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Poeciloneuron indicum include treating fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as providing relief from pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Poeciloneuron indicum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, ointments, and creams for topical application.
